intro: Dealing with aggressive hornets in Eltingville? Best Pest Control NYC provides expert hornet removal services to protect your family and property from dangerous stings. Our licensed technicians safely eliminate hornet nests from homes, businesses, and outdoor spaces throughout this residential Staten Island neighborhood.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Eltingville residents for years with fast response times and guaranteed results. Our experienced team uses safe, effective methods to remove hornet nests while minimizing risk to your family, pets, and property.
localContext: Eltingville's tree-lined streets, residential properties near Eltingville Park, and homes close to the Staten Island Railway station often attract hornets seeking sheltered nesting sites. The neighborhood's mix of single-family homes and the proximity to wooded areas make professional hornet removal essential for maintaining safe outdoor spaces.

faqs.question: How much does hornet removal cost in Eltingville?
faqs.answer: Hornet removal services in Eltingville typically range from $350 to $550, depending on nest location, size, and accessibility. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Eltingville?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Eltingville residents. Most hornet nest removals are completed within one visit, typically taking 1-2 hours depending on the complexity of the situation.

faqs.question: Are hornets dangerous to my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, hornets can be aggressive when defending their nests and deliver painful, potentially dangerous stings. Multiple stings can cause severe allergic reactions. Professional removal is the safest option to protect your family and pets.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, wasps, and yellow jackets?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than wasps and yellow jackets, typically building large, enclosed paper nests in trees or under eaves. They're more aggressive than most wasps and can sting multiple times. Our experts accurately identify the species and use appropriate removal methods.
